 {"id":"bd-3exp","title":"Add proptest property-based tests for ffs-ondisk ext4 parsing","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-25T08:50:01.903599873Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-25T10:05:17.068529325Z","closed_at":"2026-02-25T10:05:17.068504379Z","close_reason":"Added 21 new proptest property-based tests for ffs-ondisk ext4 parsing: inode field preservation, extended timestamp roundtrip, extent tree leaf/index roundtrip, extent validation (entries>max, bad magic), directory block structured roundtrip with lookup, parse/iter consistency, FileType from_raw, dir/extent checksum stamp+verify, block bitmap verification, superblock validate_geometry/v1, Ext4Extent actual_len/is_unwritten properties, ext4_chksum incremental chaining, DirEntry actual_size alignment, rec_len_from_disk identity/sentinel, superblock 64-bit block count. Also fixed xattr roundtrip test overlap bug.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ffs-ondisk","testing"]}
 {"id":"bd-3fag","title":"Add write-side checksum stamping for directory and extent blocks","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-03-21T17:06:43.001218656Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-21T17:06:52.706701356Z","closed_at":"2026-03-21T17:06:52.705777116Z","close_reason":"Added stamp_dir_block_checksum() and stamp_extent_block_checksum() to ffs-ondisk. These are the write-side counterparts to the existing verify_* functions, needed for ext4 metadata_csum compliance when creating/modifying directory and extent blocks. Both functions are exported from the crate public API. Added 2 round-trip tests proving stamp→verify correctness. 355 ondisk tests pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["checksum","ondisk","parity"]}
 {"id":"bd-3ff","title":"Perf: Add criterion benches for cache hot paths + parsing loops","description":"Goal: measure the actual hotspots once correctness fixtures exist.

Deliverables:
- Criterion bench for:
  - ARC cache hit path
  - ARC cache miss path (no I/O in bench; use in-memory ByteDevice)
  - ext4 group desc iteration / inode read (once implemented)

Acceptance:
- Benches run in CI or at least locally with stable results.
- Bench outputs are used to drive targeted optimization work.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:30:56.866258166Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T21:00:07.922658284Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T21:00:07.922640581Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["io","perf"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3ff","depends_on_id":"bd-5iz","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:31:10.503270335Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-3g31b","title":"add mounted-path ext4 EXT4_IOC_SETFLAGS coverage for COMPR enable and rejection paths","description":"Survey finding: `OpenFs::set_inode_flags` has real ext4 `EXT4_COMPR_FL` semantics in `crates/ffs-core/src/lib.rs`: it rejects COMPR when the COMPRESSION incompat feature is absent, rejects enabling it on non-regular or non-empty files, and seeds the e2compr defaults when the feature is present. The mounted-path `EXT4_IOC_SETFLAGS` E2E currently proves only generic user-flag roundtrips, so this operator-visible branch remains unproven through FUSE.

Scope:
- Add a writable ext4 mounted-path scenario that enables `EXT4_COMPR_FL` on an empty regular file in an image with the COMPRESSION incompat bit set.
- Prove that a subsequent write/read roundtrip takes the expected e2compr path without regressing existing file data or flag masking.
- Add a mounted-path rejection scenario for at least one invalid COMPR enable case (missing feature bit or non-empty file) with deterministic errno / message assertions.
- Update parity notes if the public `SETFLAGS` contract now includes e2compr-specific behavior.

Acceptance:
- Mounted-path `SETFLAGS` coverage reaches the COMPR-specific success path and at least one rejection path.
- Tests prove the flag transition and post-ioctl file behavior, not only the raw ioctl return value.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"ubuntu","created_at":"2026-04-21T07:29:19.182951077Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T04:38:19.915292320Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"bd-3g31b","title":"add mounted-path ext4 EXT4_IOC_SETFLAGS coverage for COMPR enable and rejection paths","description":"Survey finding: `OpenFs::set_inode_flags` has real ext4 `EXT4_COMPR_FL` semantics in `crates/ffs-core/src/lib.rs`: it rejects COMPR when the COMPRESSION incompat feature is absent, rejects enabling it on non-regular or non-empty files, and seeds the e2compr defaults when the feature is present. The mounted-path `EXT4_IOC_SETFLAGS` E2E currently proves only generic user-flag roundtrips, so this operator-visible branch remains unproven through FUSE.

Scope:
- Add a writable ext4 mounted-path scenario that enables `EXT4_COMPR_FL` on an empty regular file in an image with the COMPRESSION incompat bit set.
- Prove that a subsequent write/read roundtrip takes the expected e2compr path without regressing existing file data or flag masking.
- Add a mounted-path rejection scenario for at least one invalid COMPR enable case (missing feature bit or non-empty file) with deterministic errno / message assertions.
- Update parity notes if the public `SETFLAGS` contract now includes e2compr-specific behavior.

Acceptance:
- Mounted-path `SETFLAGS` coverage reaches the COMPR-specific success path and at least one rejection path.
- Tests prove the flag transition and post-ioctl file behavior, not only the raw ioctl return value.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"ubuntu","created_at":"2026-04-21T07:29:19.182951077Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T21:54:00.000000000Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T21:54:00.000000000Z","closed_reason":"completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"bd-3geu","title":"Source parity report from FEATURE_PARITY.md in harness","description":"Eliminate duplicated hardcoded parity counts in ParityReport::current by parsing FEATURE_PARITY.md, keeping harness/CLI parity output anchored to one source of truth.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-25T20:14:14.491080127Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-25T20:36:11.388600708Z","closed_at":"2026-02-25T20:36:11.333626060Z","close_reason":"Completed: parity report now sourced from FEATURE_PARITY.md with summary-section-only parser + tests","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-3gj","title":"FUSE: wire read-only xattr ops to FsOps","description":"Context: bd-1x8 is deletion-gated; this is the next non-destructive, high-value gap.\n\nScope:\n- Implement FUSE callbacks for read-only xattr operations in crates/ffs-fuse/src/lib.rs.\n- Route to existing FsOps listxattr/getxattr methods in crates/ffs-core/src/lib.rs.\n- Add/extend tests for expected behavior and errno mapping.\n\nAcceptance:\n- listxattr/getxattr paths work through FUSE adapter for FsOps implementations that already support them.\n- Existing commands/tests continue passing (fmt/check/clippy/test).\n- Coordinate file scope via Agent Mail.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"TurquoiseFox","created_at":"2026-02-11T18:41:44.610934120Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T18:47:03.974741351Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T18:47:03.974719981Z","close_reason":"Implemented FUSE read-only xattr wiring and passed all required gates","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","fuse","xattr"]}

 {"id":"bd-rzk9","title":"Per-core steal threshold must be positive/finite","description":"PerCoreDispatcher::should_steal divides by steal_threshold without sanitizing. Add sanitization + tests.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-11T19:17:12.392008213Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-11T19:22:09.813042670Z","closed_at":"2026-04-11T19:22:09.812786836Z","close_reason":"Completed: sanitize per-core steal threshold","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

-{"id":"bd-s35v4","title":"add FS_IOC_GET_ENCRYPTION_POLICY_EX (v2) ioctl support","description":"Survey finding: ffs-fuse implements FS_IOC_GET_ENCRYPTION_POLICY (v1 policy, 0x400C6615) but not the newer FS_IOC_GET_ENCRYPTION_POLICY_EX (0xC0166616) which supports v2 policies with additional fields (contents_encryption_mode, filenames_encryption_mode, master_key_identifier).

Scope:
- Add FsOps::get_encryption_policy_ex() trait method with default fallback
- Define FS_IOC_GET_ENCRYPTION_POLICY_EX constant and fscrypt_get_policy_ex_arg struct
- Parse on-disk fscrypt_context_v2 from xattr if present
- Wire dispatch in ffs-fuse ioctl handler with size/version validation
- Add unit dispatch tests for v1-only, v2, and unencrypted cases
- Add mounted-path E2E test on encrypted ext4 image if available

Acceptance:
- FS_IOC_GET_ENCRYPTION_POLICY_EX returns v1 or v2 policy depending on on-disk format
- Direct dispatch tests verify struct encoding and version negotiation
- Unencrypted files return ENODATA cleanly","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T11:23:10.609876351Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T14:59:49.586809670Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"bd-s4eea","title":"add mounted-path ext4 read-only EROFS coverage for create mkdir link and symlink","description":"Survey finding: ext4 fuse_e2e proves create, mkdir, hard-link, and symlink success on writable mounts, while ffs-fuse already hard-rejects these mutations with EROFS on read-only mounts. Scope: add mounted-path ext4 probes that attempt create, mkdir, hard_link, and symlink on a read-only mount and assert exact EROFS with unchanged directory contents. Acceptance: ext4 FUSE E2E freezes read-only operator-visible refusal for these entry-creation mutations.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"ubuntu","created_at":"2026-04-21T08:25:48.819671875Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T05:26:00Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T05:26:00Z","close_reason":"Stale in-progress sweep: no commit reference after more than 12 hours; closed per operator instruction so the work can be re-filed or reclaimed cleanly.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
